Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Wesley Pratt, Esq. (415)268-8000 for Defendant Encore Karaoke. Elinor Leary, Esq. (415)673-4800 for Plaintiff, Jonathan Korzekaw. Joseph May, Esq. joseph@josephmaylaw.com for Plaintiff Jonathan Korzekaw.

The Court stated that this case management conference is on calendar to address the August 24th letter received by Mr. Pratt, that the trial date is set for October 13, that arbitration hearing was not previously mentioned and addressed the issue of the expert witness Mr. Kevheart. Furthermore the Court stated that it understands that Mr. Kevheart is moving but finds it hard to believe that he would be unavailable via Zoom for the entire month of October and that it is willing to take him out of order to accommodate his schedule. Mr. Pratt provided reasons as to why he is requesting the trial date be moved and stated that if the date remains the same then he will not be available. Ms. Leary opposed any further continuance of the trial date. The Court stated that there is no good cause to grant the continuance. Mr. Pratt questioned if each and every witness will testify via Zoom. The Court stated yes, that it believes that this is the best way. Ms. Leary questioned if the Court will hear motions in liminie before October 13 and reported that briefing has not been completed. All parties agree that reply briefs are not needed.

The Court set the following deadlines: September 24, 2021 for moving papers to be filed and served and October 1, 2021 for any opposition papers to be filed and served. The hearing on the motions in limine is set for October 08, 2021 at 11 a.m. in Dept 210 hearing.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: N. Wong. Matter Not Reported. (D210)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And For Appointment Of Discovery Referee And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Jonathan Korzekwa And His Counsel Joseph S. May And The Law Office Of Joseph S. May By Defendant Encore Karaoke Lounge, Llc
Off Calendar Mar-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kevin Labarbera, Esq. of DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY, LLP, T:415.268.8000, for defendants/ moving party;
Joseph S. May, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF JOHN S. MAY, T:415.781.3333, for plaintiff.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel requested clarification as to the timing and scope of further deposition of Mr. Latu. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentatiave ruling on DEFENDANTs ENCORE KARAOKE LOUNGE, LLC, MARK ALOIAU, ANTHONY AMBURN's Motion For Protective Order And For Appointment Of Discovery Referee And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Jonathan Korzekwa And His Counsel Joseph S. May And The Law Office Of Joseph S. May By Defendant Encore Karaoke Lounge, LLC as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel Oceanic Latu to answer questions at deposition is granted. Defendant's motion for a protective order is denied. Defendant's motion for appointment of a discovery referee is denied. Both parties' requests for monetary sanctions are denied.

Defendant's position conflates discoverability and admissibility. The fact of the witness's prior felony conviction and the details of that crime may or may not be admissible at trial, either to impeach the witness's credibility or to establish a material fact at issue. (Evid. Code 1101(b); Hassoldt v. Patrick Media Group, Inc. (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 153, 165 fn. 11 ["the principles enunciated in [People v. Ewoldt (1994) 7 Cal.4th 380] are as applicable to a civil case involving the admissibility of prior uncharged acts as they are to a criminal case involving the same issues."].) However, plaintiff is entitled to discovery regarding the underlying facts, which may determine their admissibility.

Order signed; stipulation for protective order was also signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
